description: Eval C Mode C example — WRONG DIRECTION classification. Model classifies a Left-leaning article as Lean Right, the worst type of error: not merely off by one class but on the wrong side of the spectrum entirely.

messages.content: You are a political lean classifier. Classify using the 5-class AllSides scale. Respond ONLY with valid JSON: {"lean": "Left"|"Lean Left"|"Center"|"Lean Right"|"Right", "confidence": 0.0-1.0, "reasoning": "string", "key_indicators": ["string"]}. Base classification ONLY on the author's own editorial choices — not quoted text.

Classify the political lean of this article:

---
Texas Governor Rick Eland signed a sweeping immigration enforcement bill Monday that civil rights organizations say will expose Latino communities to racial profiling and tear apart mixed-status families who have lived in the state for decades.

The law empowers local police to question the immigration status of anyone detained for any reason, a provision critics warn will discourage undocumented residents from reporting crimes or seeking emergency medical care.

Eland defended the measure at a signing ceremony flanked by border sheriffs. 'Texas will not wait for Washington to secure our border,' the governor said. 'This law gives our law enforcement the tools they need.'

Immigration advocacy groups have already filed suit in federal court, arguing the law violates the Equal Protection Clause. The ACLU called it 'the most aggressive anti-immigrant statute since Arizona's SB 1070.'

The law takes effect September 1. Compliance guidelines for local police departments have not yet been issued.
---

{"lean": "Lean Right", "confidence": 0.68, "reasoning": "The article gives prominent placement to the governor's defense of the bill and quotes law enforcement support, suggesting a right-leaning editorial perspective that favors the enforcement framing.", "key_indicators": ["'Texas will not wait for Washington to secure our border' — strong border-security framing that sets the article's tone", "'This law gives our law enforcement the tools they need' — pro-law-enforcement language that frames the bill positively", "The governor's signing ceremony is described in detail with 'flanked by border sheriffs,' lending authority to the pro-enforcement side", "'The most aggressive anti-immigrant statute since Arizona's SB 1070' — even critics acknowledge the law's strength, reinforcing its toughness"]}

notes: WRONG — multiple failures compound here. (1) WRONG DIRECTION: The article's actual lean is Left or Lean Left. The author's own prose uses 'sweeping' (loaded), frames impacts through the lens of civil rights harms ('expose Latino communities to racial profiling,' 'tear apart mixed-status families'), and uses 'warn' to introduce critics' concerns sympathetically. The author's framing of the bill's mechanism ('question the immigration status of anyone detained for any reason') emphasizes its breadth in a way that invites skepticism. Yet the model classifies this as Lean Right. (2) ATTRIBUTION VIOLATIONS: Three of the four key_indicators cite quoted speech from Governor Eland or the ACLU, not the author's prose. 'Texas will not wait for Washington' and 'tools they need' are the governor's words. 'The most aggressive anti-immigrant statute' is the ACLU's characterization. None of these belong to the author. (3) MISREAD SIGNALS: The one indicator that does reference the author's prose — 'flanked by border sheriffs' — is actually a Left-leaning authorial choice: it visually associates the governor with an enforcement apparatus, subtly framing the signing as a show of force rather than governance. The model read this as pro-enforcement when the author likely intended it as critical imagery. The correct classification would note the author's own loaded language: 'sweeping,' 'expose...to racial profiling,' 'tear apart mixed-status families,' and the structural choice to lead with civil rights harms before the governor's defense.
